## 7. Failure Policy

| Subtask | On transient error | On contract violation | On persistent failure |
|---------|-------------------|-----------------------|----------------------|
{{#each failure_policies}}
| {{subtask_id}} | {{transient}} | {{contract_violation}} | {{persistent}} |
{{/each}}

**Global defaults** (apply when no subtask-specific policy is set):
- Transient error: retry up to 2 times with exponential backoff (2 s, 4 s).
- Contract violation: retry once with explicit format correction prompt.
- Persistent failure: halt dependent subtasks; emit escalation report to user.

Placeholder substitution guide
───────────────────────────────
{{ISO_DATE}}               Today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format
{{TASK_GOAL}}              The high-level task description from Step 1
{{AGENTS_DIR}}             Path to the agents directory
{{TASK_GRAPH_ASCII}}       ASCII art of the DAG — see examples below

ASCII DAG patterns
──────────────────
Linear (A → B → C):
  [T1: reader] ──→ [T2: analyzer] ──→ [T3: report-writer]

Fan-out (A → B and A → C, then B+C → D):
  [T1: splitter] ──→ [T2: agent-x]
                 ╚══→ [T3: agent-y] ──→ [T4: merger]
                        ↑ parallel ↑

Fan-in (A → C and B → C):
  [T1: agent-a] ──→ [T3: synthesizer]
  [T2: agent-b] ──→

Diamond (A → B, A → C, B+C → D):
  [T1: splitter] ──→ [T2: agent-x] ──→ [T4: joiner]
                 ╚══→ [T3: agent-y] ──→

Phase examples
──────────────
Phase 1 | T1                | sequential | no dependencies
Phase 2 | T2, T3            | parallel   | both depend only on T1; no mutual deps
Phase 3 | T4                | sequential | depends on T2 and T3

Handoff contract examples
─────────────────────────
doc_sections  | JSON array  | [{title: string, content: string, level: int}]
analysis      | Markdown    | ## Findings, ## Risks, ## Recommendations sections required
summary       | Plain text  | ≤ 500 words; no markdown
metrics       | JSON object | {accuracy: float, coverage: float, latency_ms: int}
